newsSince: No net-new material news since June 9; the ~-14% move is a pullback from the prior rerating. May revenue +43.65% YoY was already captured in the June 9 case, and Q1 2026 results (record 37% gross margin, EPS +101% YoY, AI data-center capex from top-4 CSPs up ~60%) pre-date the window. Delta showed 800VDC power/liquid-cooling/microgrid solutions at NVIDIA GTC 2026.

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha vs passives lens: no new evidence since June 9; the -14% is a rerating pullback, not a thesis change, so do not up-rank on the fall. Already tagged crowded/rerated; at ~$198B cap and +109% YTD, under-owned residual upside stays minimal. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Rubin-era rack power and AI server power shelves with top-4 CSP capex up ~60% YoY.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentally strong but fully recognized; crowded/rerated benchmark with shrinking residual alpha.
verdicts.bear: ~$198B cap leaves little under-owned upside; cyclicality if AI capex moderates.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent/under-covered lens: Delta is deeply relevant to data-center power and cooling but is no longer an undiscovered supplier, and nothing since June 9 changes its coverage status. The -14% pullback is not new fundamental evidence. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Power & cooling leadership levered to AI data-center buildout and liquid cooling.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap, highly tracked; little latency left to exploit.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI server power, liquid cooling, industrial automation

verdicts.rankRationale: Master under-owned-bottleneck list: no new evidence since June 9 (May +43.65% YoY already captured); the -14% pullback is rerating noise, not deterioration, so hold rather than up-rank on the fall. Power/cooling bottleneck exposure intact but recognition is high at +109% YTD.
verdicts.bull: Scarce grid/power/thermal bottleneck exposure with record 37% GM and strong AI-driven revenue acceleration.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck scarcity offset by high recognition and a steep rerating.
verdicts.bear: Valuation prices much of the AI-power upside; margin/pricing execution must keep pace with capex.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Power supplies, thermal management, grid/switchgear demand
